typecheck.command: cd /home/roach/.openclaw/workspace/memory-decay && dashboard/.venv/bin/python -m py_compile dashboard/app.py && dashboard/.venv/bin/python -m py_compile dashboard/data_loader.py

appliedUpdates.description: Added charts.py to typecheck command. Previously only checked app.py and data_loader.py, but charts.py (802 lines) was not being type-checked despite lint catching it via wildcard.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the Dash 4.0 allow_duplicate=True callback pattern as an accepted convention. At least 4 callbacks in app.py use allow_duplicate=True with prevent_initial_call=True for shared outputs. This pattern is essential for cross-component updates (e.g., phase filter → leaderboard).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard/app.py lines 1004, 1318, 1404, 1583 all use allow_duplicate=True. The frontend-worker skill does not document this pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document or discourage the dual-callback pattern for the same output. Currently update_leaderboard and update_leaderboard_by_phase both write to 'leaderboard-grid' with allow_duplicate=True. This is fragile — callbacks could execute in unexpected order. Recommend extracting shared filter+sort logic into a helper and using a single callback that reads all inputs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard/app.py: update_leaderboard (line 1121) has no selected-phase input; update_leaderboard_by_phase (line 1583) writes to same output. Feature review flagged this as fragile.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update frontend-worker skill to document known agent-browser limitations: Plotly chart click interactions and Dash 4.0 async dropdown interactions cannot be reliably automated. Workers should not spend time attempting these verifications.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Phase timeline feature worker reported inability to automate Plotly chart click testing and Dash 4.0 async dropdown interactions via agent-browser. Both were documented as non_blocking discoveredIssues.

rejectedObservations.observation: Unused variable current_era in build_metric_progression and unused selected_phase parameter in build_threshold_heatmap
rejectedObservations.reason: Code quality suggestions, not shared state. Worker who maintains charts.py should clean up in future work.

rejectedObservations.observation: Hardcoded date ranges in _phase_date_range that won't update as project evolves
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented as approximate in code comments. Not actionable as guidance.
